claimText: Ways and Means Advances (WMA) are temporary advances provided by the RBI to central and state governments to manage temporary mismatches between receipts and payments.
evidenceExcerpt: Ways and Means Advances (WMA) are temporary advances given by the RBI to the central and state governments to cover mismatches in receipts and payments.
examUse: Statement-based question to test the fundamental purpose of WMA.
distractorHooks: WMA is provided by commercial banks.
WMA is available only to the central government.

claimText: The authority for the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) to provide Ways and Means Advances (WMA) is derived from the RBI Act, 1934.
evidenceExcerpt: Authorized by the RBI Act, 1934, WMAs are not a source of financing the fiscal deficit. Legal framework: RBI Act, 1934 authorises the central bank for WMA.
examUse: Correct-pair question matching the financial instrument with its authorizing legislation.
distractorHooks: Authorized by the Banking Regulation Act, 1949.
Authorized by the Government of India Act, 1935.
Authorized by an annual Finance Ministry notification.

claimText: Ways and Means Advances (WMA) are not intended to be a source for financing the government's fiscal deficit.
evidenceExcerpt: WMAs are not a source of financing the fiscal deficit. Unlike ad hoc Treasury Bills, the instrument of WMAs is not a source of financing fiscal deficit.
examUse: Statement-based question to differentiate WMA from deficit financing tools.
distractorHooks: WMA is the primary tool for financing the fiscal deficit.
WMA and ad hoc Treasury Bills serve the identical purpose of deficit financing.

claimText: The interest rate charged on Ways and Means Advances (WMA) is the RBI's repo rate.
evidenceExcerpt: The interest rate is the RBI's repo rate. Interest rate on WMA is the RBI’s repo rate.
examUse: Statement-based or correct-pair question linking WMA to the repo rate.
distractorHooks: The interest rate is the reverse repo rate.
The interest rate is the bank rate.
The interest rate is a fixed rate set by the government.

claimText: Ways and Means Advances (WMA) must be fully repaid within three months from the date they are issued.
evidenceExcerpt: the advance must be repaid within three months. It shall be fully paid off within 3 months from the date of making the WMAs.
examUse: Statement-based question testing the tenure of WMA.
distractorHooks: Repayment period is 91 days (to confuse with T-bills).
Repayment is linked to the end of the financial year.
